The author is dealt two cards from a standard 52-card deck. To calculate the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards, we can use the complement rule. The total number of ways to choose 5 cards from the deck is $ \binom{52}{5} $. The number of ways to choose 5 cards without any hearts is $ \binom{36}{5} $, since there are 36 non-heart cards. The probability of no hearts in the 5 cards is $ \frac{\binom{36}{5}}{\binom{52}{5}} $. The probability of at least one heart is then $ 1 - \frac{\binom{36}{5}}{\binom{52}{5}} $, which can be expressed as a combinatorial function and simplified to a closed form.

***  

The author has two strategies to consider. For Strategy A, the expected value is calculated as $ 0.4 \times 1000 + 0.6 \times (-500) = 400 - 300 = 100 $. For Strategy B, the expected value is $ 0.6 \times 800 + 0.4 \times (-200) = 480 - 80 = 400 $. The expected value of Strategy B is higher, so the author should choose Strategy B. 

Bag of knowledge, bag of wisdom, bag of luck, bag of chance, bag of cards, bag of life.
